CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-28-2023 Continued To Oct-25-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jul-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Leo D. Plotkin, Esq., in court, Levy, Small & Lallas, (310) 471-3000, for Plaintiffs.

Jonathan Gaskin, Esq., via Zoom, Kaufhold Gaskin Gallagher LLP, (415) 602-3559, for Defendant.

RE: Specially appearing Defendant RICHARD BORENSTEIN's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

Ruling - ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The "motion to quash service of summons and complaint against specially appearing defendant Richard Borenstein" is granted. (CCP 418.10.) Plaintiff purports to have served Borenstein via a front desk guard at a San Francisco apartment building. However, Borenstein declares under penalty of perjury that he does not live or work in the apartment, did not authorize anyone there to accept service and for "the past five years, and currently, I live outside the United States in Israel." Plaintiff's points - much of them hearsay of which judicial notice cannot be taken - are unavailing. It is true, as plaintiff says, that a security guard who denies access to a defendant's residence may be served, but it has not been demonstrated that the San Francisco apartment is Borenstein's current residence.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)

***After hearing an order is signed. (See scanned order for details)
